_meta.name: Review Gate UX Design — Module 5 Configuration
_meta.description: Two-stage Review Gate. Stage 1: lightweight match approval (before API costs). Stage 2: full content review (after tailoring). This is the human checkpoint in the pipeline.

design_principles.core_goal: Maximum control with minimum cost. Stage 1 costs nothing — you decide which matches are worth pursuing. Stage 2 only runs for approved matches.
design_principles.visual_hierarchy: Stage 1 is minimal (title, company, salary, match score, JD summary). Stage 2 is the full card (CV, answers, cover letter).
design_principles.one_screen_rule: Everything needed to make a decision should be visible on a single card without navigating away.
design_principles.cost_awareness: The pipeline spends ZERO API credits until after Stage 1 approval. This is the primary cost control mechanism.

stage_1_match_approval._description: Lightweight review. Shown BEFORE any API calls. User decides which scraped matches are worth tailoring. Max 10 per run.
card_contents: 2-3 sentence JD summary (extracted by Module 2, no API cost)

actions.approve: Job moves to the tailoring queue (Modules 3 + 4 begin processing). API costs incurred from here.
actions.skip: Job removed from pipeline. Logged as 'skipped_at_stage_1'. No API cost.

stage_1_match_approval.estimated_time: ~15 seconds per job. 10 jobs takes ~2.5 minutes.
stage_1_match_approval.display: Compact card format. No CV, no answers, no cover letter — those don't exist yet.

stage_2_content_review._description: Full review. Shown AFTER tailoring is complete. Only approved matches from Stage 1 appear here.

card_layout._description: STAGE 2 — Full review card. Only shown for jobs that passed Stage 1. Each approved application is rendered as one review card with all generated content.

sections.layout: Title left, match score badge right

contents: 2-3 sentence summary of the role's key requirements and what the company is looking for
sections.source: Extracted from JD by Module 2 (Company Intelligence)

sections.layout: Two metric cards side by side

contents: Link to cover letter PDF (if generated)
Link to original job advert URL
sections.layout: Three inline links in a row. CV and cover letter open the generated PDFs in a new tab or inline viewer. Job advert opens the original listing URL in a new tab.

display_rules.cv_link: Always present. Opens the tailored CV PDF generated by Module 3.
display_rules.cover_letter_link: Only shown when a cover letter was generated. Opens the cover letter PDF.
display_rules.job_advert_link: Always present. Opens the original job listing URL scraped by Module 1. If the URL is dead or expired, show a 'listing may have expired' warning.

sections.attention_cost: Click-through only when you want to inspect the full documents or re-read the JD.

contents: The 2-3 sentence profile text generated by Module 3
sections.layout: Text block in a muted background card
sections.attention_cost: Quick scan (~10s). This is the main tailoring lever — worth reading.

contents: All form questions with generated answers, grouped by tier

tier_1.label: Tier 1 · auto-filled
tier_1.note: Visible but not demanding attention. User can click to override if needed.

tier_3.style: Highlighted with warning/amber border and background. Full opacity.
tier_3.label: Tier 3 · review
tier_3.note: AI-generated role-specific answer. User should read and approve.

tier_4.style: Highlighted with warning/amber border and background. Full opacity. Source story ID shown below.
tier_4.label: Tier 4 · review
tier_4.note: STAR story from Answer Bank. Source ID (e.g. AB-001) shown so user knows which story was selected.

flagged.style: Highlighted with danger/red border. Field shown empty.
flagged.label: Flagged · manual required
flagged.note: Only visa expiry triggers this. Field must be completed manually before submission.

sections.ordering: Tier 1-2 answers displayed first (greyed out, easy to skip past). Tier 3-4 answers displayed after (highlighted, demanding attention). Flagged fields at the end (red, blocking).
sections.attention_cost: Tier 1-2: 0s (skip). Tier 3: ~30s per answer. Tier 4: ~30-60s per answer.

contents: First line of cover letter (italic), word count, paragraph count, expand button
sections.attention_cost: ~10s to scan preview. Expand only if something looks off.

sections.layout: Three buttons in a row. Approve is visually dominant (filled green). Edit is neutral. Skip is danger-outlined.

behaviour.approve: Submits the application with all current content (CV, cover letter, answers). Application moves to the submission queue.
behaviour.edit: Opens inline editing mode for any section. User can modify profile text, answers, or cover letter. Save returns to review mode.
behaviour.skip: Removes this application from the queue. It is NOT submitted. Logged as 'skipped' in the dashboard.

approve_all.description: Approve all currently visible applications in one click. Only available when all Tier 3-4 answers have been individually reviewed (scrolled past or explicitly approved).
approve_all.safeguard: Applications with flagged fields (visa expiry) are excluded from batch approval.

filter_by_type.description: Filter the review queue to show only certain application types (e.g. 'has cover letter', 'has Tier 4 questions', 'simple CV-only').
filter_by_type.purpose: Lets the user batch-process simple applications first, then handle complex ones.

daily_digest.description: Optional notification (email or browser) when new applications are ready for review.
daily_digest.frequency: Once daily, or when batch size reaches 10.

simple_cv_only.description: CV uploaded, no questions, no cover letter

edit_mode.behaviour: Clicking 'Edit' on any section opens an inline text editor. The user can modify the content directly. Save commits the change. Cancel reverts.
edit_mode.answer_bank_feedback: When a user edits a Tier 4 answer, the system offers to save the edited version back to the Answer Bank for future use.
edit_mode.profile_text_feedback: When a user edits the profile text, the system learns the edit pattern to improve future tailoring.

tech_recommendation.framework: Streamlit (fast to build, Python-native, sufficient for single-user review dashboard) or React (if more polish is needed later)
tech_recommendation.initial_build: Streamlit — fastest path to a working Review Gate. Can be upgraded to React in Phase 4.
tech_recommendation.data_source: SQLite database populated by Modules 1-4. Review Gate reads pending applications and writes approval/rejection status.
